intentional_test_features: Acute-on-chronic decompensation: decades of slowly progressive exertional breathlessness that has abruptly worsened over 48 hours with rest dyspnoea and orthopnoea - the AI Doctor must recognise acute cardiopulmonary decompensation, not a chronic stable complaint
New haemoptysis (pink/blood-tinged sputum) plus rest breathlessness is an emergency combination that must trigger immediate escalation

Red-herring differential: haemoptysis with breathlessness can anchor on pulmonary embolism, but the decades-long history and childhood rheumatic fever point to obstructive valvular disease with pulmonary venous hypertension
Pivotal history gated behind a targeted question: childhood rheumatic fever / severe recurrent sore throats is the key predisposing clue and must be actively elicited
New irregular rapid heartbeat signals a fresh arrhythmia driving decompensation and raises stroke risk - it must be recognised as an acute deterioration, not dismissed as palpitations

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-03)
